text: I am so happy today, everything went perfectly!
I feel so sad and alone, nobody understands me.
So angry at my boss for being unfair during the meeting.
I'm so anxious about the interview tomorrow, can't sleep.
So tired I can barely keep my eyes open.
Just thinking about what I want from life.
I am not sad at all, things are actually going well.
notes: Negation test: 'not sad' should not trigger sadness

text: I'm not happy about the grade I received.
notes: Negation test: 'not happy' should flip to sadness

text: I can't stop smiling, got the promotion I wanted!
Missing my grandmother terribly, she passed away last month.
Furious that my roommate betrayed my trust again.
The pressure at work is overwhelming, I feel trapped.
Completely exhausted after pulling an all-nighter.
Spent the evening reflecting on my choices this year.
Had a good lunch but work was stressful today.
notes: Mixed emotion: clause-aware negation should isolate stress to second clause

text: I don't feel stressed today for the first time in weeks.
notes: Negation: 'don't feel stressed' should not trigger apprehension

text: People keep pushing my buttons and I'm about to snap.
My mind won't stop racing with worst-case scenarios.
My body feels like it's made of lead today.
Woke up feeling positive and energized this morning.
ugh
fine.
I'm not angry anymore, we talked it out and I feel better.
I can't find any joy in the things I used to love.
Got bad marks in maths, my parents will be so upset.
Exam tomorrow and I haven't started studying yet.
Topped the class in physics, feeling amazing!
I threw my phone across the room in frustration.
I haven't felt anger like this in years.
Nothing makes me excited anymore.
I feel relieved the project is done but exhausted from it.
I screamed at my manager for assigning more work.
The loneliness is crushing me tonight.
What if everything goes wrong at the presentation?
Running on empty after a week of no sleep.
I'm not okay with how they handled the situation.
Feeling content and peaceful sitting by the window.
I feel like a failure in every aspect of my life.
My heart races every time I think about the exam.
Collapsed on the couch the moment I got home.
Life is strange, neither good nor bad right now.
So pissed off at the system, nothing ever works.
Dancing in my room because I nailed the presentation!
The emptiness inside me keeps growing every day.
I keep checking my phone obsessively waiting for results.
Even simple tasks feel impossible when I'm this tired.

Feeling tired and lonely, wish someone was here.
Oh great, another Monday.
Three exams in two days, I'm completely burned out.
I feel like I'm walking on eggshells all the time.
My daughter took her first steps today and I cried happy tears.
I sat through an entire dinner listening to my relatives criticize every choice I've ever made.
The grief comes in waves. Some days I think I'm doing better and then a song plays and the pain crashes back.
I stare at my phone waiting for the callback. The uncertainty is eating me alive.
Three consecutive night shifts this week and my body is officially giving up.
Walking through the old neighborhood today stirred up a complicated mix of feelings.
I'm not as stressed as I thought I'd be.
Finally cleared my backlog and feel so relieved!
My blood is boiling after that argument with my sibling.
I woke up at 3am in a cold sweat again. The nightmare about the accident keeps coming back.
My brain is foggy and I can't concentrate on anything.
Got appreciated at work today and it made my day.
I'm not going to let them get away with this.
I feel hollow inside, like nothing matters anymore.
The fear of failure is stopping me from even trying.
I'm running on caffeine and prayers at this point.
Reading a book and pondering the meaning of it all.
I scored 95% on my board exams, I'm so proud!
The grading system is completely biased and I'm furious.
Failed the entrance exam, all my preparation was wasted.
Board exams in two weeks and I'm panicking.
Studied all night for the test and now I'm a zombie.
My heart is full of joy after seeing my friends.
I despise the toxic environment at my workplace.
I regret not saying goodbye when I had the chance.
I'm terrified of public speaking and I have to do it Friday.
Dragging myself through the day with zero motivation.
I'm not happy but I'm not sad either, just existing.
Love spending time with family but they drain my energy.
notes: Mixed: joy + fatigue, clause-aware should separate

text: I won't tolerate this disrespect any longer.
